exec_summary: GrowthBook had a strong week overall, with clicks up 36% (3,501 to 4,775) and impressions up 13% (103,247 to 116,772). However, the homepage (/) drove the majority of that gain, adding 1,143 clicks on its own -- likely branded search or a direct traffic surge -- so the underlying content-driven growth is more modest. The comparison page /compare/growthbook-vs-statsig was a bright spot, jumping from 1 to 9 clicks, while the LaunchDarkly comparison page dipped 5 clicks and should be monitored.

The most important strategic finding this week is that GrowthBook has zero AI Overview citations across all tracked keywords, and AI referral traffic from ChatGPT and Perplexity is effectively flat. As AI-generated answers increasingly absorb top-of-funnel queries around A/B testing and feature flags, being absent from those citations is a growing revenue risk. Building structured, definition-forward content that mirrors how AI systems synthesize answers should be an immediate priority.

No competitor content surges or new publications were detected this week in the feature flagging and experimentation space. Optimizely and Amplitude Experiment both have high-opportunity pages on experimentation messaging and Bayesian statistics respectively that are outranking GrowthBook, but there is no new activity to flag. The competitive landscape was quiet, which makes this a good week to close content gaps rather than react.
content_recommendations: 1. Write a dedicated guide on 'Bayesian Statistics for A/B Testing' targeting the keyword Amplitude Experiment ranks for (KD 22, composite score 75). This is a Quick Win opportunity -- low difficulty, high relevance, and GrowthBook's statistical engine is a core differentiator that should own this conversation.

2. Expand the /compare/growthbook-vs-statsig page now while momentum is building (clicks grew 8x WoW from 1 to 9). Add a detailed feature comparison table, pricing breakdown, and a section on statistical methodology differences to capture users actively evaluating alternatives.

3. Refresh the /compare/growthbook-vs-launchdarkly page, which dropped from 7 to 2 clicks this week. It is GrowthBook's most commercially important comparison page and should not be losing ground -- audit for freshness, add updated pricing, and ensure it targets 'LaunchDarkly alternative' queries explicitly.

4. Write a 'Feature Flag Testing' guide to compete with Flagsmith's ranking content (composite score 70, KD 0). A KD of zero means this is essentially uncontested -- GrowthBook should be able to rank quickly with a thorough, developer-oriented post.

5. Optimize the /blog/how-to-a-b-test-ai-a-practical-guide/ post, which lost 7 clicks this week (9 to 2). Given the rising interest in AI experimentation, this page should be gaining traffic, not losing it -- review for keyword targeting gaps and add structured definitions that improve AI Overview citability.
